item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, same. I actually started drawing them together a while back

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I feel like this SCP could be great friends with SCP-3009.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel sorry for the sapient Instagram account. :(

Also, I really like this presentation of a Keter that isn't necessarily hostile or dangerous, yet still fully deserves its classification. Aki doesn't want to hurt anyone, and probably couldn't physically harm anyone even if it wanted to, but if left unchecked it would probably have "eaten" all of Instagram, bringing it to global attention and probably breaking the Foundation's veil.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, looking back I see you're right in that I misunderstood 'not being real' with 'wanting to be real', but that wasn't the main point of my criticism. My main gripe was the emphasis on emotion over events. I wouldn't say I was making those comparisons to criticise that it is not those stories, it was to show the need for substance in addition to the emotion, the emotion being part of a greater whole. Those stories acted as vessels for the emotion/morals contained within, they weren't just feels. Sure, this is condensed storytelling, but condensed doesn't mean cut down.

Just so I'm not being entirely negative, I'll say that I'd like this story a lot more if things happened between the conversations. The conversations are where the emotion happens but the events are the anomalous effects Aki causes, so I would have liked if that was developed more, because then I would feel stuff is really happening. I said before that it was like a friendship story at 10 times the pace, putting developments in would pad it out nicely. But that's just my thoughts, well done on the scip anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there. One thing I noticed is that you do a lot of comparisons, and seem to be criticizing this for not being those stories. The storytelling isn't going to be the same as Pinocchio or Frankenstein or Blade Runner, (I'd argue Frankenstein is not a good comparison) because it isn't those stories. The story isn't that he wants to be real. It's Aki realizing that he isn't real. I do appreciate your criticism, but I wanted to point that out, as you seem to have misinterpreted the story. And yes, this story relies on emotion. It's not a novel or movie, so story telling will be slightly condensed in comparison.

That's just my response to your criticism. Thanks for the read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(breathes)
Alright, time to be 'that guy'.

So. Anime boy AI is sad and wants to be real. And… not much else.

Do I feel sad for him? Yes.
Do I feel this is worthy of my upvote. Not really, no.
Why? Honestly, it doesn't feel earned.

Full Review (quite long)
I hope I don't look like a heartless bastard for this

The core idea here is that an AI/machine is self-aware and wants to be real. That's been done a bajillion times already, from Pinocchio to Frankenstein to Blade Runner. That itself isn't a problem but there needs to be something new to it, which is something I don't see. Oh for sure the format of it is very unique, the storytelling through social media screenshots is brilliant, and the effort to do so highly commendable. But that's just how the story is told, the icing on the cake, it's the cake and the story itself I have issue with here.

This article leans heavily on the emotional side of things. So heavily, in fact, that other components like how it affects other accounts become forgotten by the end. The story itself amounts to: AI thinks he's real, realises he's not real, gets sad, but then believes in himself. Touching, but not an interesting story. I've seen it before, but in Pinocchio there was a relationship between creator and creation, in Frankenstein there was that and a moral about the cruelty of man, and in Blade Runner there was a future-noir adventure story and reflections on what makes us human. The emotion was part of greater whole, which in turn strengthened the parts. Here it's just the emotion, which on it's own doesn't make for a good story.

Speaking of just the emotion, I think this encapsulates the problems I have. Only 4 pictures in and Aki says:

All I wanted to do was make friends.

Touching, but I barely know him. This continues.

Dex, why aren't you like the others?

You never wanted to be my friend?

You mean it?

I believe our friendship wasn't fake.

And more. All very soft and sweet, but I'm not attached to these characters yet, so it just comes off as sentimental. It's like watching an Oscar-winning romance film and skipping to the emotional speech at the end; it just doesn't pay off without the rest of the story. There's a touching friendship story here, but sped through at 10 times the pace it should be. I'm more interested in the effects on other accounts. What happens to them? How does Aki feel about it, seeing as he doesn't mean to? Can he control it, and if so what else can he control? How does he feel about the Foundation blocking him? How was he created?

I almost feel bad writing this much criticism seeing all the effort and passion that clearly went into this, but… -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: haha, you get a star sticker

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or should we say… mew-ltimedia?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Baby boy. I would like to protect him…

Saw this on featured and took a shine on it. +1!

item.title: Really good
item.content:encoded: This is a great example of a Keter that is not inherently deadly, but has the likelihood to become something destructive. Really like the narrative of this one, but suddenly deciding to tell the skip that he is not real does not seem like something the foundation would do. +1 anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are correct. I'm legally only allowed to write multimedia articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mew you have cemented yourself as the multimedia article writer. There is no going back from this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love light-hearted SCP like this one, but novoting. The premise is cool but the execution was a bit off for the same reason others with critique mentioned. It's strange to suddenly ask about personal details rather than just have it do certain tasks. How does the Foundation suddenly figure out that 5554 isn't human?

The reveal feels unnatural unlike a lot of "alternate perspective" SCP's like 085 and 2639 for this reason, especially with 2639 where the dialouge is similar but the information they and the Foundation gain is more clear and logical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi Aki! You seem sweet!

obvious joke out of the way, this was cute! i like cute scips. i also like when the Foundation is nice to scips. if Aki had a body, i'd want to hug him. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the sad little baby ai boy :( +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You my friend just caught an error! I totally did not mean to set addendum 3 before addendum 2. it does canonically take place after it. I have no idea how that happened. I'll see if I can fix that.

Anyway, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seeing a light-haired anime guy with the name Akihiko brought back memories of Persona 3.

I do have a small critique of the timeline of things. The posts from Addendum 1 are Aki's posts from July - September 28, 2019; Addendum 2 is the first contact and is November 8, 2019; and Addendum 3 is on September 30, 2019. Taking these times to be accurate it means that Addendum 3 happens before Addendum 2. When I was just reading the first time I thought Addendum 3 took place after Addendum 2 and feel it would work better like that.

I find 5554 to compliment 085 in a few ways. We know a lot about how Cassy was made but don't get a long interview with her, meanwhile we get a long interview with Aki and not a lot of backstory. Both approaches to non-human anomalies are interesting to me. +1

item.title: Re: It's pretty good
item.content:encoded: Well in the beginning he's supposed to be pretending to be a typical twenty year old, so he's not supposed to sound professional. That's likely why. Also, thanks!

item.title: It's pretty good
item.content:encoded: It's pretty good but i feel like something is wierd. Maybe it's that the dialogue between the "interviewer" and Aki sounds a bit wierd. The "interviewer" sounds a lil' unprofessional

item.title: Re: Never expected to get emotional over a fictional instagram account on the SCP wiki
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I love hearing love for wholesome stuff, it's my favorite ^^ next to internet stuff

item.title: Never expected to get emotional over a fictional instagram account on the SCP wiki
item.content:encoded:
But I'm fine with that. Kind of feel bad for Aki who's low-key having an identity crisis as an anomalous AI, although I suppose that make's them feel more human. The order of the images that shows how Aki is learning more about themselves and the deterioration of their mental state made the narrative even more interesting. Great job author.

I also really liked how this was a wholesome-ish SCP. Refreshing from all the other end-of-the-world, horror monster's that the SCP wiki is mainly comprised of (not that I dislike those).

One +1 for the poor lad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much!

Canonically, Aki. never existed, and is just a personally created by the AI, but you can think whatever you want to, I love seeing different interpretations of the story ^^

And yeah Crane is one of my nicer Personnel characters, serious and focused, but more empathetic than others, and it happens to work out given he works primarily with AI's.

Thanks again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

The formatting/effort put into this is very admirable. Is the implication that Aki formerly existed, and is now consolidated into an AI, or is that a personality entirely created by the AI? I'm not sure I picked up on that. Regardless, I enjoyed it! The Foundation allowing Crane to communicate daily with Aki is heartwarming- what a good guy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s a very brief read but pretty good, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: feel that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im sad now :( +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I quite enjoy the concept of anomalous social media accounts, so I thought I'd make another one! Yep, my first SCP was a youtube one. Also, I highly recommend checking out SCP-3009, it is absolutely amazing and was an inspiration to me. It's about a sentient snapchat account, and so much more!

item.title: Re: I like this
item.content:encoded: Who said it has to be perfect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this one, something about the anomalous social media concept caught my attention almost instantly.
Not seen much like this before, good job.

+1

item.title: I like this
item.content:encoded:
+1

However Aki is an anomalous account making anomalous effect but this features is completely not mentioned in the addendums so they seems unrelated to other parts.That's not perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At the time of that post, you were still at a -1.

And no, disagreement is not petty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you sure about that?

Not only did I change my vote, but it's equally petty to call out someone because you disagree with how they voted, let alone actively comparing user's comments with their votes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose it's a different-interests kind of thing, at least he's aware that it's reasonably immature to simply downvote something because of a style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two things here:

  1. Downvoting for anime being mentioned in an article is extremely petty and dumb, even if you are technically allowed to do it.
  2. You did not reverse your vote to a +1, your vote is still at -1 right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll admit, I downvoted initially at the first sign of anime. Totally bad form, I'm aware, but I gave this post the benefit of the doubt, powered through, and enjoyed the logs sufficiently enough to reverse my vote. I feel like the concept of "AI realizes they aren't real" has been stale for a while, and this brought new life to that concept in a very engaging way.

Also, weirdly enough, I knew an Oliver Crane in school when I was a kid, so that partially affected my decision to +1 as well.

item.title: Re: SCP-5554
item.content:encoded:
I suppose it's a different-folks-different-strokes situation, I think I just like something exciting and mysterious that I get to think about being behind an article.

As said well done on the effort, bloody love the new style, keep on going.

item.title: Re: SCP-5554
item.content:encoded: Thanks for giving your opinion. Honestly, I don't think any twist like a cult or evil corporation was needed, that's just a personal preference that it would have added anything. Maybe it's just a presence of mine that it wouldn't. I like simple, and don't like to over-complicate things. I just liked the story of a sapient AI that wanted friends that accidentally turned any account that talked to him into a husk for the price of him learning by absorbing their info. I do appreciate your opinion, though. At the end of the day I feel like this is a separation in article taste, but hey, it happens!

item.title: SCP-5554
item.content:encoded:
I can definitely appreciate the absolute herculean effort that probably went into this article, the style to me is something refreshing and new. But, apart from that, I can't really see anything more than a semi-entertaining short story.

I think it would've benefited from exploring what's behind the account, maybe it's a sinister corporation attempting to test out new technology on unsuspecting users? Maybe it's a cult attempting to bend these people's minds using such an innocent-looking account? There's a lot of opportunities just left untouched here, and what remains is really just another "Sentient AI".

To me, this article is bursting, absolutely bursting, with potential. From the format to its concept, I love it, but all that potential really just never gets used and is kind of just left to sit in the corner, taking a backseat to a dialogue that, though a good jumping point, doesn't really carry the SCP any further.

But that's just my 2-cents, good on you for putting this much work in. I hope you take my semi-stupid semi-helpful blathering into consideration.

item.title: Re: Sweet Article, +1
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much! It was quite the hassle to deal with all the pictures, but very worth it!

And yes, Aki is best boy Foundation confirmed.

item.title: Sweet Article, +1
item.content:encoded: This was a great SCP, and I think I speak for everyone when I say we appreciate the fact that you spent many hours on this. I was sort of expecting a 3101-style body transfer in the end, but overall this was a somewhat wholesome ending. Also, AI or not, Aki is good boi.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can tell you as the author he isn't lying. Aki only realized certain things because that's how he was programmed. He was programmed to act as a Japanese teenage boy, specifically the internet persona of one, which wouldn't worry about a body. I think you're overanalyzing it, but it's cool you care about it enough to try and work out the logic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's inconsistent. It really freaked out when it realized it was missing memories, but not having a body is totally chill? I don't buy that.

The only way it could be logically consistent is if the entity is fully oblivious to not having a body, which would cause it to react at least as harshly as realizing it has no memories, or its experiencing some sort of simulation-like state where, to it, its sitting at a computer typing.

Or, its lying about everything entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, the project head Researcher Crane chose to not do that because that would be a weird ass question

hey internet friend, look around and tell me what you see

Also he would just send a photo he found on instagram from one of the accounts he absorbed like he did with the dog. And no, that wouldn't drive Aki insane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite enjoy this. There is one thing missing, to me. Assuming we start with the assumption that this entity believes itself to be human, why don't we ask it to look around and describe it's surroundings? Maybe touch it's nose, or manipulate an object, take a picture..

Might drive it kind of insane.

item.title: Re: mmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmm
item.content:encoded:
Specifically:

Aki is eating the other accounts.

He's alive. Living creatures eat.

Aki eats any other account that gets too close. It's a reflex: he doesn't realize he's draining them of their contents and leaving them as empty husks, but that's what he's doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god thank you for telling me I didn't notice just removed it goddmanit some fucking people

Anyway, I'll be sure to fix that error as I had

As for that, I just kinda wanted to use the title professor to be honest. I don't really think that is a big deal honestly. Also the note's entire point is that the scp is harmless, but the foundation has some paranoia due to past experiences.

Thanks for the read, tho :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Novote for now.

There are some errors, like the lack of capitalization on one instance of "Instagram."

Why is the last note signed by someone whose primary title is Professor? Surely any instructional staff wouldn't be handling the reassignment of object classes or directing this fairly innocuous SCP in particular.

Also, as of the time of this comment's writing, someone vandalized the bottom of the page. This doesn't factor in at all to my vote, but be sure to get a mod to deal with the account who did it even if you remove the vandalism yourself.

Edit: Changing to a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!! I thought that the screen shots would add to the authenticity of the SCP. I absolutely love social media and internet SCPs, so I wanted to write another one! (My first SCP was a youtube SCP)

I’m really glad you liked the dialogue! It appears I’ve been improving my dialogue lately and that makes me so happy ;;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've seen social media skips before, but this is my favorite. Love how you used screenshots and not just plain text transcripts, and the story was a perfect balance of sad/sweet with a weird dose of existential dread.

Great work with the dialogue too! The realism of it really impressed me +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This edged into existential horror in the cutest possible way. The conundrum of a character realizing that it's a character, and plaintively seeking assurance, was well depicted.

item.title: Re: mmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmm
item.content:encoded:
it does not influence the owners, and the accounts become untraceable, leaving the foundation unable to determine it. Also, he can't write Japanese because in my canon, he was sorta born to be a Japanese teenager under the eye of an American. Also he influences the accounts by like, any accounts that interacts with him basically turns into a husk that gives him meaningless praise, which he didn't mean to do.

Also it was developed by reflecting on general teens of any language

Thanks for the feedback, and thanks for the read :)

item.title: mmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmm
item.content:encoded:
hmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmm…

I like what's going on here, and it seems perfectly SCP enough, but something here feels missing. it feels like there's too much dialogue (says me lmao) and not quite enough documentation going on, although I know that this is an scp hinging on that dialogue to explain what Aki is.

I'm left with three questions, 1) what does 5554 do to the 5554-1 accounts that causes that kind of automation, 2) does it actually affect the owners (I feel like the Foundation should be able to figure this out unless it causes the owners to become untrackable), and 3) why is Aki unable to write out Japanese if he's an AI who ostensibly picked up this persona as a Japanese teenager, which I'm assuming was developed at least in part by reflecting Japanese teenagers on instagram?

none of those questions particularly influence my vote (except maybe the second one)… which unfortunately I can't decide on as up or down. novote for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a bit, I primarily did collabs cause they helped me grow as an author so I'm experienced with how they work, so gladly lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always get so nervous to ask authors I admire to collaborate ;;

Me too, but you'd be my first in that arena so you might need to show me the ropes, haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sure! He's an interviewer in SCP-4208, he's an interviewer and project head of SCP-5090, and I plan on doing more with him, he's in a few drafts that haven't hit the light of day as well, I mostly use him in tech/internet anomalies which I've been writing more and more because I love them (Crane is my favorite character of mine that ive written so I love talking about him and am very happy with the attention he's gotten recently lol; also 4208 is old so the dialogue may not be as good imo)

And yay :) I always get so nervous to ask authors I admire to collaborate ;;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, cool, love me some recurring author characters. Would you mind providing links for Crane's appearances?

Also I'd love to collaborate if circumstances permit, thanks for the offer!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah yeah! If I do write more with him, I'll most definitely develop him more past the development done in the article. And yeah, Crane is an anomalous technology specialist so he talks to AIs all the time lol, most of which have been fairly naive, so I would love to show that development of Aki for the sake of Aki and Crane as he's a reoccurring character of mine.

If you'd ever wanna collaborate on a tale about Aki I wouldn't be opposed to it

And thanks :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's rad! Bit of advice, though, I'd suggest giving Aki more… agency? The most active thing he does in the story is piece together that his newfound friend is fake, and after the revelation of his artificiality he mostly reacts in the positive to Crane's encouragement. Which makes sense in-narrative — Aki is a kid (ayy, catgirl rights!) and Crane has probably dealt with a fair share of AIs — but I would have preferred seeing Aki either surprise Crane or have a moment of personal self-reflection.

If you do decide to continue Aki's story, I think it'll be lovely to see him mature and think on his own as he processes how other forms of intelligence react to existence — but hey, it's your story, not mine.

Best of luck!
